label: Smooth center drift — sine Lissajous replaces FBM (was jerky)
notes: FBM drift was jerky because high-frequency octaves cause sharp direction changes when traversed at speed. Replaced with two sine waves per axis with incommensurate frequencies: x = sin(t*0.17)*0.20 + sin(t*0.11+1.3)*0.14, y = sin(t*0.13+0.7)*0.22 + sin(t*0.08+2.5)*0.13. Sine is C∞ smooth — no jerk. Incommensurate frequencies mean the Lissajous path never exactly repeats. Amplitude gives ~±0.34 max drift in aspect-corrected space.
